column1: 2004-06-17‚Äé"As the shadows creep along my L shaped desk and the night air is finaly cooling off in the blackness outside
column2: I am starting to grasp that summer is here.  I will wake up in the morning
column3: and I won't be going to school.  I'll turn in my bed as the sharp rays of morning light attempt to convice me that it is a new day
column4: a day full of time that I can choose to spend
column5: or waste.  I would like to present you all with a challenge:  Look at everything differently.  I've had a few people talk to me about ""creativity
column6: and that they could never see something the way certain people can
column7: I encourgingly disagree.  Being creative is not something a person is born with
column8: it's a choice to make the most of what you have.  The principles in this have spilt over all areas of my life
column9: leaving long stains of color and diference.  Choose to see things differently.  Sometimes the best way to start is to excercise the mind. Close your eyes
column11: open your eyes and think of five oberservations about that object.  Even if you point at a wall
column12: there are more than five oberservations you can pull from a wall.  It would be my desire that nobody could say about this summer
column14: there is always something to do
column16: strengthen the brain.  -To God be the glory"
